items.headline: U.S. Equities Show Mixed Performance Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Key Earnings
items.summary: U.S. equities closed Monday on a subdued note, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite posting modest gains and reaching new record highs, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a slight decline. Overall market sentiment reflects caution as investors balance improving geopolitical signals with lingering uncertainties surrounding the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict, which continues to impact oil prices. Strong technology earnings and the persistent 'AI theme' are providing underlying resilience and driving momentum in certain sectors. Pre-market futures for Tuesday showed the Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 futures slightly down, while Dow futures were up.

items.headline: Sandisk Surges on Strong Performance
items.summary: Sandisk Corporation ($SNDK) notably gained 8.1%, contributing to the positive movement in the technology sector.

items.headline: Cleveland-Cliffs Jumps on Rare-Earth Optimism
items.summary: Cleveland-Cliffs ($CLF) saw an 8.71% increase, closing at $10.61, boosted by optimism surrounding rare-earth materials and geopolitical developments.

items.headline: Fastly Gains Ahead of Earnings
items.summary: Fastly ($FSLY) rose 8.59% to $25.80 as investors repositioned ahead of its upcoming earnings report.

items.headline: Nvidia Continues AI-Driven Ascent
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) jumped 4%, extending its recent strength and pushing its market capitalization above $5 trillion, underscoring continued AI leadership.

items.headline: Intel Rises Amid Upbeat Earnings and AI Demand
items.summary: Intel Corporation ($INTC) added 3% on the day, with its shares also noted for a substantial 93% return over the last month and trading at 52-week highs, fueled by strong quarterly results and AI demand.

items.headline: Domino's Pizza Falls on Disappointing Sales
items.summary: Domino's Pizza experienced an 8.8% decline following disappointing first-quarter sales results.

items.headline: Spotify Profit Forecast Out of Tune
items.summary: Spotify ($SPOT) saw downward pressure as its profit forecast disappointed investors, despite hitting 293 million subscribers.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Begins Two-Day Meeting Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve commenced its two-day policy meeting today, April 28, 2026. The central bank is widely anticipated to maintain the federal funds rate unchanged within its current range of 3.5% to 3.75%. This meeting marks Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's final rate decision before his term expires on May 15. The FOMC's statement and Powell's press conference on Wednesday will be closely watched for insights into the Fed's stance on inflation, particularly given the elevated energy prices stemming from the U.S.-Iran conflict.

items.headline: Technology Sector Continues to Lead, Driven by AI Momentum
items.summary: The technology sector remains a significant driver of market performance, fueled by robust demand for AI products and strong earnings. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) has achieved an impressive 18-day winning streak, having surged 44% since March 31, significantly contributing to the sector's gains. This leadership, however, highlights an increasing market concentration.

items.headline: Key Companies Release First Quarter 2026 Earnings
items.summary: Several major companies reported their first-quarter 2026 results today: S&P Global ($SPGI) announced its Q1 performance. United Parcel Service ($UPS) reported consolidated revenues of $21.2 billion and diluted EPS of $1.02, reaffirming its full-year guidance. JetBlue Airways Corporation ($JBLU) delivered strong Q1 revenue performance exceeding expectations. Centene Corporation ($CNC) posted adjusted diluted EPS of $3.37, better than anticipated, and raised its 2026 guidance. Corning Incorporated ($GLW) announced strong Q1 core sales growth of 18% and core EPS growth of 30%, driven by Gen AI and new solar products. General Motors (GM) raised its adjusted-profit guidance, while BP's profits doubled due to higher oil prices. Hilton Worldwide also boosted its 2026 growth projections.

items.headline: Iran Conflict Pushes Oil Prices Higher, Affecting Global Markets
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions stemming from the U.S.-Iran conflict and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz continue to exert upward pressure on oil prices. Brent crude was up 2.81% to $111.27 per barrel, and WTI crude gained 3.12% to $99.38 early Tuesday. This situation is a key factor in inflation concerns and broader economic uncertainty.

items.headline: U.S. Private Employers Add Jobs in Early April
items.summary: Preliminary estimates from the ADP National Employment Report for April 28, 2026, indicate that U.S. private employers added an average of 39,250 jobs per week for the four weeks ending April 11, 2026. This data provides an ongoing look at the health of the labor market.
